description: BANT + behavioral + property-fit scoring. Used by lead-qualifier and agents/lead-scorer. Scores are additive to a max of 100. Tiers: >=70 hot, 40-69 nurture, <40 long-cycle.

budget_capacity.description: B in BANT. How well their stated budget matches listing range from property.json.

authority.description: A in BANT. Decision maker signal from form/message.

need_specificity.description: N in BANT. How specific is their interest?

timeline.description: T in BANT. Urgency of decision.

behavioral.description: What they did, not what they said.

source_quality.description: Which channel produced this lead? Organic outperforms cold ads.

audience_segments.description: Match a lead to one of these segments based on budget + interest + message. Used by follow-up skill for multivariate content selection.

segments.pitch: residency process + title clarity + tax + legal
broad features then ask for qualifying details

initial_phase.description: Strategy B: intent-only tiering for first submission. The landing form collects only name, phone, interest — no budget, timeline, or message body. First-submission scoring ceiling is ~40 under the full rubric, which misclassifies hot buyers. Initial phase uses interest + phone presence to assign a contact-SLA tier independently of the score. The full-rubric score is still computed and stored for the quality gate (scaling decisions). Post-contact rescoring uses the full rubric with data gathered during outreach.

initial.description: First form submission. Tier is determined by intent signals, not score. Score is still computed for quality-gate purposes.

hot_if.note: Initial-phase hot: high-intent interest + phone provided. Full score deferred to post-contact.

nurture_if.note: Initial-phase nurture: exploratory interest but reachable by phone.

initial.fallback_note: Initial-phase long-cycle: no strong intent signal or no phone.

post_contact.description: After first outreach. Use full BANT + behavioral rubric with data gathered during contact.
